Title: Former Kedah deputy speaker shared final dinner after daughter's engagement
Article:
ALOR STAR: Nur Anisa Mohd Jamil's last memory of her father was sharing dinner after her engagement ceremony, a day before he was found drowned today.
The daughter of former Kedah deputy speaker Datuk Mohd Jamil Md Idross described him as a devoted father who spent much of his time at home and at the mosque after retiring from politics.
Nur Anisa, 27, the youngest child of Mohd Jamil, said her father appeared healthy and in good spirits during her engagement ceremony yesterday.
"He was able to see me get engaged at a ceremony held yesterday, and he looked healthy.
"Last night, my sister, my fiancé and I had bihun sup with my father at home. He ate with a very good appetite. That was the last meal we shared," she said.
Mohd Jamil, 78, a former Pengkalan Kundor assemblyman, was found drowned in Sungai Teluk Bagan in Tandop today.
Nur Anisa said her father had largely withdrawn from public life after leaving politics, preferring to spend his time gardening, attending the mosque and being with his family.
"After dinner last night, I told him that I had locked all the doors before going to bed, and he said it was okay.
"But at about 5am today, my mother, sister, and I realised he was not in the house.
"We looked for him at the mosque because we thought he might have gone there for dawn prayers, but he wasn't there.
"We continued searching and eventually saw his Perodua Kancil parked by the river, along with his slippers nearby," she said when met at the Forensic Department of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital.
She said the family was shocked by the tragedy and never imagined they would lose their loved one so suddenly.
Nur Anisa said the family frequently took Mohd Jamil on holidays, with their most recent trip being to Kuala Lumpur on May 30 to celebrate her mother's birthday.
"He won't be able to see me get married next January, but I'm grateful that he was there to witness my engagement yesterday," she said.
Meanwhile, his wife, Datin Noor Hasita Mat Isa, 58, who is Kedah Wanita Umno secretary, said her husband had dedicated more than four decades to Umno and public service.
She said he served two terms as Pengkalan Kundor assemblyman from 1995 to 1999 and from 2004 to 2008, before becoming Kedah deputy speaker from 2018 to 2023.
"He looked healthy and showed no signs of illness. We never expected that he would leave us so suddenly," she said.
Noor Hasita said the funeral prayers and burial would be held at the Teluk Bagan Mosque Muslim Cemetery in Tandop before Asar prayers today.
Earlier, authorities confirmed that
Mohd Jamil was found drowned in Sungai Teluk Bagan
at about 8.10am.
His body was recovered by personnel from the Jalan Raja Fire and Rescue Station and later handed over to the police for further action.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jaslinda found note left by mountain guide during search for her
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, found a note left behind by rescuers at Kem Air Busuk on Gunung Batu Putih while search and rescue (SAR) operations were underway.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said Jaslinda brought the note out with her and showed it to firefighters yesterday after she was rescued.
"The note was left by mountain guide Hafis at Kem Air Busuk while he was conducting a search in the area.
"At the time, search efforts were focused on the route between Pos Musoh and Kem Air Busuk on May 30.
"Jaslinda only reached the area yesterday," he said.
Jaslinda was found
after being missing for 14 days during a hiking expedition at Gunung Batu Putih in Tapah, Perak, and is currently staying at the Tok Batin's house in Kampung Lubuk Gaharu.
The matter was confirmed by Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon.
Jaslinda had joined a group of 13 others and two Forestry Department mountain guides on the Trans Spencer Chapman expedition, which began at 2am on May 23 via the Pos Gedung–Gunung Bah Gading–Gunung Batu Putih–Kuala Woh route.
She and another hiker, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, had earlier experienced health issues and stopped hiking.
However, the supply chain executive at a private company reportedly continued towards the summit and was last seen on May 24.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bankrupt at 28: Former CEO rebuilds life after business collapse
Article:
SHAH ALAM: "I have always prayed to Allah SWT to take my life once all my debts are cleared," said Muhd Wafiq, 33, who was once a millionaire and a chief executive officer (CEO) at a young age before going bankrupt at 28 after falling victim to betrayal.
As a result, his interior design and cabinet installation company was forced to shut down, causing him to lose the luxury he once enjoyed.
Despite the hardship, he had no choice but to rebuild his life and earn a living. He took on various jobs, including selling durian by the roadside, selling frozen food, working as a cattle caretaker, a p-hailing rider, and a car salesman.
All of this was done to survive and support his wife, who was then pregnant with their first child.
Today, Muhd Wafiq is still bankrupt, but his life is more stable. He now owns a vehicle and lives in rented accommodation after his home was auctioned off by the bank two years ago.
Sharing his story, Wafiq, who holds a Bio-Composites degree from Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM), said he started a business after graduating and once employed dozens of staff.
"Later, I was tested after dealing with a party that failed to settle payment for a project. I also suffered losses of up to RM300,000 while seeking justice in court, but that was the price I had to pay, including losing a company that once generated up to RM12 million in annual profit."
Wafiq, a father of two, said his lowest and most painful moment came when he was financially strained to the point of being unable to pay his employees' salaries.
"It's upsetting because one of my staff asked for a salary of more than RM10,000 for the Raya period, but I could only afford to give RM500. He accepted it with patience, but I felt sorry because he did not deserve to be in that situation.
"Even now, I still owe some of my staff money, but they have never demanded it. I did not have time to dwell too long or fall into depression then, because all I could think about was what I could do to earn money," he said, adding that his family was aware of what happened but chose not to burden them.
He worked hard to earn a living through his own efforts, even though it was not easy starting from scratch after coming from a comfortable phase of life.
"It's not easy, but what happened taught me to fully surrender to Allah SWT and realise that work, money and what we have do not guarantee tomorrow.
"Everything happens by Allah's permission, and what has happened in my life is what is best from Him.
"I am at peace now with the journey I have gone through. Perhaps Allah SWT has destined my life this way so that I can share it and make it a lesson for others, as I too made mistakes in the past," he said.
Wafiq now earns a living as an independent consultant and handles marketing work for companies that require his services.
He also shares his experience with business owners facing difficulties to help them recover from setbacks.
"My intention in sharing my story on Threads recently was to give encouragement to business owners who are going through a phase of discouragement. I try to offer free guidance based on my own experience.
"My services for external companies are also meant to help ensure they do not end up like me. I hope my sharing is beneficial, and I am now striving to become a better husband, father, son and servant of God."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OPEC+ set for fourth oil quota hike since Hormuz closure
Article:
LONDON: OPEC+ is set to agree on Sunday a fourth increase in oil output targets in as many months, three OPEC+ sources said, even though the US war with Iran is still preventing several of the group's members from pumping more.
The war has cut oil flows via the Strait of Hormuz, creating the world's biggest ever supply crisis as key OPEC+ members including Saudi Arabia have been unable to supply customers in full since the end of February. The crisis for OPEC+ deepened when the United Arab Emirates left the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries after almost 60 years.
Seven core members of OPEC+, which groups OPEC and allied producers including Russia, have increased their output quotas from April to June by almost 600,000 barrels per day.
In reality, the group's production has collapsed due to export cuts by Gulf members, averaging 33.19 million bpd in April versus 42.77 million in February, according to OPEC figures.
On Sunday, the seven members will likely increase targets by about 188,000 bpd from July, the sources said. This is the same as the June hike, which was adjusted down from monthly increases of 206,000 bpd in May and April to take into account the UAE exit.
All the sources spoke on condition of anonymity and said a final decision had not been made.
The seven of 21 OPEC+ members due to meet on Sunday are Saudi Arabia, Iraq, Kuwait, Algeria, Kazakhstan, Russia and Oman.
A full OPEC+ ministerial meeting is also scheduled for Sunday but is not expected to make any policy changes, the sources said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cosmetics exports top US$5.6bil in Jan-May, Seoul's top consumer export item
Article:
SEOUL: South Korea's outbound shipments of cosmetics jumped sharply from a year earlier in the first five months of the year, making beauty products the country's top consumer export item, Yonhap News Agency reported, citing government data.
Combined outbound shipments of Korean beauty products came to US$5.6 billion during the January-May period, up from US$4.6 billion recorded a year earlier, according to data released on Sunday by the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Resources.
The ministry said demand for South Korean cosmetics has continued to expand in Europe and the United States, despite a decline in exports to China, traditionally one of the country's largest overseas markets.
Notably, cosmetics became the top export item among the country's five major consumer goods categories -- agricultural and fishery products, cosmetics, fashion apparel, household goods and pharmaceuticals.
Over the past three years, agricultural and fishery products had consistently ranked as the largest export category among the five consumer goods sectors.
South Korea's cosmetics exports surpassed the US$10 billion mark for the first time in 2024, before reaching an all-time high of US$11.4 billion in 2025.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas to decide on Bersatu ties tomorrow, says Hadi
Article:
MARANG: Pas will decide on its political cooperation with Bersatu tomorrow, says party president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang.
He said the matter will be discussed at the Pas central committee meeting following deliberations by the party's Syura Council.
"We will discuss and make a decision, especially regarding our relationship with Bersatu, at the Pas central committee meeting tomorrow," he told a press conference on Friday.
Hadi said the Syura Council had decided to leave it to the central committee to make a final decision and announce the party's next steps.
Separately, the central committee meeting is expected to deliberate on the number of seats Pas will contest in the upcoming Johor state election and the logo it will use.
The Marang member of parliament confirmed that Pas would field candidates in the Johor polls, adding that the exact number of seats would be finalised after a briefing from Johor Pas.
In May, Hadi announced that Pas would review its alliance with Bersatu, citing actions that allegedly violated the spirit of camaraderie within Perikatan Nasional.
This includes Bersatu's purported move to block the admission of Malay-Muslim parties into the opposition coalition.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar fields questions on AI, leadership, and education at AIMST University dialogue
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged universities to produce graduates who not only excel academically but are also guided by strong values, ethics and social responsibility.
Speaking during the "Temu Anwar" programme at AIMST University, he addressed students' concerns on education access, artificial intelligence (AI), leadership and national unity, while stressing the importance of balancing academic excellence with humanity and compassion.
Responding to a question on how young people can become agents of change while rejecting corruption, Anwar said students must first master their chosen fields while remaining sensitive to societal issues.
"Universities must strive for excellence, but excellence alone is not enough. We need excellence with soul," he said.
Anwar said Malaysia must continue building expertise in emerging fields such as AI, information technology, quantum computing, blockchain and energy transition to remain competitive in the global economy.
He added that universities and academics must continuously adapt to technological developments, particularly in AI and digitalisation.
Drawing on former Harvard University dean Harry Lewis' book, Excellence Without a Soul, Anwar said academic achievement should be accompanied by humanity, compassion and concern for wider social issues.
He reminded students not to lose sight of challenges outside campus, including poverty, inequality and racial divisions.
On concerns about access to professional programmes such as medicine, dentistry, pharmacy and other health sciences courses, Anwar acknowledged the financial burden faced by students from lower- and middle-income families.
He said the government would continue providing support through education financing, targeted assistance and other initiatives, while balancing competing national priorities.
"We will continue to work hard and do our best to help every single student in this country," he added.
Addressing questions on AI, Anwar highlighted initiatives such as AI for Rakyat and programmes conducted through the National Information Dissemination Centres (Nadi) to expand digital literacy and awareness nationwide.
He said early exposure to AI was crucial to prevent a widening gap between urban and rural communities.
Anwar, however, stressed that technological advancement must be guided by ethics and human values.
"AI is important, but we must ensure that humanity, justice and values remain part of the equation," he said.
When asked about the qualities required of future leaders, Anwar said young Malaysians should cultivate integrity, humility, sincerity and a lifelong commitment to learning.
He said leadership should be rooted in strong character, knowledge and a genuine desire to serve society.
"Education aims to acquire knowledge to disseminate justice and truth," he said.
Anwar also expressed optimism about the younger generation, describing their questions as thoughtful and forward-looking.
"I am confident we are on the right track and moving forward because of the young people we have," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Porsche crashes into Penang hotel lobby
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A 61-year-old woman lost control of her Porsche and crashed into a hotel lobby along Persiaran Gurney this afternoon.
Northeast police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said initial investigations indicate that the 11.15am incident occurred when the driver, who had just visited her family, mistakenly stepped on the accelerator while attempting to drive home.
This caused the vehicle to ram into the hotel lobby.
"No fatalities or injuries were reported. The extent of losses is still under investigation," Abdul Rozak said in a statement.
The case is being investigated under Section 43 of the Road Transport Act for careless and inconsiderate driving, an offence punishable by up to 12 months in jail and a maximum RM10,000 fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GRS welcomes Upko's application to join coalition as positive development
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) has described the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation's (Upko) application to join the coalition as a positive development and a strong vote of confidence in its leadership and direction.
GRS secretary-general Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said Upko's bid reflected growing support for efforts to unite Sabah-based parties under a common platform.
He said the application, submitted on May 29, would be tabled for consideration at the coalition's Supreme Council meeting on June 18.
"In principle, Upko's intention to join GRS is viewed as a very positive development in efforts to unite local parties with grassroots support under one coalition umbrella," he told a press conference after launching the Ihsan Food Bank (i-FB) initiative today.
Armizan said Upko had formally submitted its application letter to GRS chairman Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor through party president Datuk Ewon Benedict and its senior leadership.
He said he was currently conducting engagement sessions with Upko leaders to gather additional information for a paper to be presented to the Supreme Council.
Armizan also pointed to Upko's decision to back Hajiji as chief minister following the last Sabah election as evidence of the party's longstanding commitment to working alongside GRS.
"The immediate and voluntary support extended by Upko to Hajiji after the state election demonstrated the party's sincerity in being with GRS from the outset," he said.
He noted that Upko was not a new political party but one with a long history of championing Sabah's rights and interests.
Armizan said the key consideration would be Upko's commitment to GRS's four core principles — Sabah First, Sabah Forward, Sabah Prosper and Sabah United.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MIPP confirms BN invitation, to respond at 'appropriate time'
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) has confirmed receiving an invitation to join Barisan Nasional, though no decision has been made yet.
MIPP president P. Punithan said the party is open to reviewing its future based on current political developments.
"We will respond to the invitation to join BN at an appropriate time," he told the New Straits Times when contacted.
"Any decision will be made only after our central committee discusses it. At the moment, MIPP's election machinery is preparing for the Johor and Negeri Sembilan polls," he said.
Pressed on whether the invitation came from BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Punithan responded only with a smiling emoji.
Yesterday, FMT, quoting sources, reported that Ahmad Zahid and Punithan had held talks, and that MIPP was invited to join BN.
MIPP joined Perikatan Nasional in April 2024 after its application was endorsed by the coalition leadership.
Its entry was seen at the time as strengthening PN's outreach to Indian voters, an area where the coalition previously had limited formal representation through component parties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Survivor recalls horrific Gerik highway crash ahead of first anniversary
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: "June 9 is a date I will remember for the rest of my life. It was the day 15 of my university friends died before my eyes."
These were the words of Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) student Abdul Wafiy Kamaruddin, 24, who survived the bus crash along the East-West Highway near Gerik, Perak, that claimed the lives of 15 students on June 9 last year.
The final-year Bachelor of Art Education student said the tragedy remains etched in his memory, even as the first anniversary of the accident approaches.
"I remember everything clearly. We were all in high spirits when we boarded the bus in Jerteh to return to campus. But fate took a tragic turn in Gerik.
"When I regained consciousness, my friends' bodies were scattered around me. Some were screaming for help, and others were crying in pain from their injuries," he said.
"I will remember every moment for the rest of my life. Praise be to God, I only sustained minor injuries in the tragedy," he said when met at the UPSI Tautan Kasih dan Ukhwah event with families of the 2025 Gerik incident victims here today.
Also present was UPSI vice-chancellor Professor Datuk Dr Md Amin Md Taff.
Wafiy said the incident left him traumatised whenever he used public transport, particularly buses.
"Following the incident, my injured friends and I underwent counselling sessions provided by the university, and some are still receiving counselling today.
"Thankfully, my emotional condition and trauma have improved, and I am grateful to UPSI for always looking after us," he said.
In the accident at 1.10am on June 9 last year, 15 UPSI students were killed when a bus carrying students from Jerteh, Terengganu, to the university's main campus in Tanjung Malim collided with a multi-purpose vehicle.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CIMB sees Malaysia growing above 4.0pct despite Middle East risks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is expected to sustain economic growth above four per cent this year despite escalating tensions in the Middle East, supported by resilient domestic demand, investment activity and electronics exports.
CIMB Securities economists Chew Khai Yen and Michelle Chia maintained their 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) growth forecast of over four per cent, citing a robust investment pipeline and continued demand for AI-related electronics exports.
Following a meeting with policymakers, industry representatives and investment agencies on May 20-21, Chew and Chia said the base-case scenario continues to project GDP growth of 4.3 per cent in 2026, even as higher energy prices create challenges for selected sectors.
The outlook assumes the conflict in the Middle East remains contained, with oil prices staying elevated through the third quarter before gradually easing.
Under this scenario, economic growth is expected to slow to 3.7 per cent in the third quarter (Q3) before recovering to 4.0 per cent in the final quarter of the year.
"Oil prices remain elevated through Q3 before easing gradually, fuel subsidy adjustments are measured and both key growth engines, electronics and household consumption, remain intact.
"Growth troughs at 3.7 per cent in Q3 before recovering to 4.0 per cent in Q4," they said.
A stronger-than-expected de-escalation could lift growth to 4.8 per cent, while a prolonged conflict coupled with weaker electronics demand may drag growth to 3.8 per cent.
Chew and Chia said Malaysia entered the current period of uncertainty from a position of strength.
Economic growth in the first half of 2026 is expected to average above 4.9 per cent, supported by solid household spending, ongoing investment activity and healthy exports from the electrical and electronics (E&E) sector.
Policymakers reportedly continue to view a full-year growth range of four per cent to five per cent as achievable, while considering a sub-four per cent outcome unlikely unless a significantly larger external shock emerges.
The main risk stems from higher energy costs flowing through the economy via several channels.
The most immediate impact is being felt in petroleum-related manufacturing industries.
While businesses have largely secured alternative sources of crude oil and refined products, higher input costs are weighing on profitability and production decisions.
Data already show signs of weakness.
Output in refined petroleum, chemicals and plastics manufacturing contracted in the first quarter of 2026, highlighting the strain facing sectors heavily dependent on energy and petrochemical inputs.
Higher fuel and transportation costs are also expected to gradually squeeze household purchasing power and corporate margins.
Headline inflation is expected to peak at 2.8 per cent in the fourth quarter before moderating in 2027, resulting in full-year inflation of 2.3 per cent.
Private consumption, which accounts for roughly 60 per cent of Malaysia's economy, is projected to moderate to 4.3 per cent growth this year from 5.3 per cent in 2025.
The slowdown is expected to be concentrated in discretionary spending rather than essential purchases.
Small and medium enterprises, particularly in retail, logistics and services, are seen as among the most vulnerable as they face rising operating costs while possessing limited ability to pass those costs on to consumers.
Agriculture could also face pressure from rising fertiliser costs and potential supply constraints.
Industry feedback suggests ammonia- and potassium-based fertilisers are becoming more expensive, which could affect yields later in the year.
The anticipated arrival of a super El Niño event may further compound challenges for crop production.
Despite these headwinds, Chew and Chia believe Malaysia's diversified economic structure will continue to provide resilience.
The country's E&E sector remains a key bright spot, with industry participants reporting full order books and no significant cancellations.
Demand linked to AI infrastructure and data centres continues to support export growth, with analysts estimating that data centre-related activity accounts for 40 per cent to 45 per cent of Malaysia's E&E exports.
"The data centre investment cycle itself appears set for a sustained multi-year run. The same analyst characterised the current phase as a 'golden period' of investments, with a committed project pipeline extending well into next year and the cycle potentially lasting through 2028.
"This structural demand underpins our expectation for construction growth of 7.0-8.0 per cent in 2026," they said.
Chew and Chia noted that private investment approvals reached a record RM427 billion in 2025, with an estimated 90 per cent of approved projects progressing as planned.
Gross foreign direct investment inflows have also remained close to RM100 billion for three consecutive quarters.
They also expect the federal government to achieve its fiscal deficit target of 3.5 per cent of GDP despite higher fuel subsidy costs, aided by stronger petroleum-related revenue and continued efforts to improve spending efficiency.
With growth remaining resilient and inflation pressures viewed as temporary, Chew and Chia expect Bank Negara Malaysia to keep the overnight policy rate unchanged at 2.75 per cent throughout 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'No such thing': Loke rules out PH-BN cooperation in Negri polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pakatan Harapan (PH) will not cooperate with Barisan Nasional (BN) in the upcoming Negri Sembilan election, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said.
"No such thing. I think our position is very clear. Umno wants to contest all the seats in Johor, while PH wants to contest all the seats in Negri Sembilan. So it's tit for tat," he said when asked about the possibility of an electoral pact between PH and BN.
Loke, who is also the transport minister, was speaking to reporters after the launch of a documentary in conjunction with DAP's 60th anniversary.
He was joined by the party's national chairman Gobind Singh Deo, deputy secretary-general Hannah Yeoh, Federal Territory chairman Tan Kok Wai, Ipoh Timor member of parliament Howard Lee and Bangi MP Syahredzan Johan.
Loke said the circumstances in Negri Sembilan made any talk of electoral cooperation with BN untenable, pointing to the role their assemblymen played in triggering the dissolution of the state assembly.
"Because in Negri Sembilan it's not tenable for us to talk about cooperation. The whole idea of this dissolution is because the Umno assemblymen
pulled their support
.
"How can we then say right now we are going to cooperate with them to avoid any clashes?" he said.
He said the cleanest solution was to put the decision back in the hands of voters.
"The best thing to do is to return the mandate to the people, let the people decide who they want to run the state government, either BN or PH. Very clear," he said.
However, he said, the decision to go head-to-head in both states would not affect the working relationship between PH and BN at the federal level, highlighting that the unity government remained intact and functional.
"As far as we are concerned, the relationship at this time is still a working relationship and we are still running the government together.,
"Of course, we do not want to jeopardise the situation at the federal level. The cabinet still functions as normal," he said.
Earlier this week, BN chairman
Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said
the coalition remained open to continuing its cooperation with PH, although it was too early to discuss any electoral arrangement.
On May 25, Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said Johor BN reaffirmed its intention to contest the state election independently for all seats.
Meanwhile, on June 4, Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said PH would contest all 36 state seats, after announcing the dissolution of the state legislative assembly, which took effect the next day.
The last Johor election was held on March 12, 2022, called early by then menteri besar Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad following the dissolution of the state assembly on Jan 22.
BN won a landslide victory, capturing 40 out of 56 state seats.
PH won 12 seats, while PN secured three seats and Muda one.
The Johor assembly would have automatically dissolved in April next year if elections were not called earlier.
In the 2023 Negri Sembilan election, a PH-BN alliance formed the government after securing 31 of the 36 seats in the assembly.
PH won 17 seats, while BN secured 14. PN won the remaining five seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DAP to contest 17 seats in upcoming Johor election, says Loke
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP will contest 17 seats in the upcoming Johor election, including the 14 seats it contested in 2022 and three additional constituencies.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said DAP would defend the 10 seats it won in the last state election, as well as the four seats it contested but failed to capture, while seeking three more constituencies this time.
He said one additional seat was virtually finalised but would not be announced yet as negotiations between coalition partners PKR and Amanah were ongoing and expected to be concluded within the next day or two.
"I can safely say that DAP will be contesting 17 seats in the coming state election in Johor," he told reporters.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz: Johor gearing up for city-wide transformation ahead of RTS Link launch
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor caretaker government has unveiled five key measures aimed at ensuring the smooth implementation of the Johor Baru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link.
Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the state government was taking a comprehensive approach to prepare Johor Baru for the anticipated influx of commuters and the significant changes in traffic movement expected once the RTS Link goes into full operation on Jan 1 next year.
Among the measures are to strengthen pedestrian connectivity through the construction of pedestrian overhead bridges linking RTS Bukit Chagar with major activity centres in the city.
This is expected to enhance the RTS Link and other public transport services integration by implementing smarter traffic management systems, expanding the park-and-ride concept, and reorganising traffic flow around key congestion hotspots.
The strategy is to connect Johor Baru and Singapore seamlessly, which is expected to transform the people's movement landscape as a whole.
"All these efforts are the state government's continuous commitment to ensure the city centre is fully prepared to welcome the RTS and maximise its benefits for Bangsa Johor," he said in a statement.
Under the first initiative, pedestrian access near the RTS Bukit Chagar station will be strengthened through dedicated overhead walkways to improve connectivity and encourage greater use of public transport.
The second measure focuses on integrating the RTS Link with existing rail and public transport services, including the Electric Train Service (ETS), the Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd Komuter, public buses, taxis and shuttle bus networks to create a seamless travel ecosystem.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/02/1383944/kempas-baru-pasir-gudang-ktm-komuter-begin-operation-mid-year
Onn Hafiz said the state government will also introduce smarter and more structured traffic management strategies, including contra-flow lanes and upgraded intelligent traffic light systems, to improve vehicle movement around the city centre.
The park-and-ride concept will be expanded to encourage commuters to leave their vehicles at designated locations before continuing their journey via public transport, reducing traffic volumes entering Johor Baru's central business district.
Traffic flow near major bottlenecks, particularly near JB Sentral and Jalan Jim Quee, will also be reorganised to ensure smoother and more efficient vehicle circulation.
The RTS Link is one of the most significant bilateral infrastructure projects between Malaysia and Singapore. Stretching approximately four kilometres, the rail system will connect Bukit Chagar in Johor Baru with Woodlands North in Singapore.
Construction of the project has progressed steadily and remains on track to meet its year-end completion deadline.
Upon full operation, the RTS Link is expected to carry up to 10,000 passengers per hour in each direction, or as many as 40,000 passengers daily in its initial phase, significantly easing congestion at the Causeway, one of the world's busiest land border crossings.
The project is also expected to spur economic growth, improve labour mobility, attract investment and strengthen Johor Baru's position as a regional economic hub closely linked to Singapore.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Blaming everything on DAP is Rafizi's 'new syndrome'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has hit back at Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli for claiming the party was behind the dissolution of the Negri Sembilan state assembly.
Loke said the former PKR deputy president had developed a "new syndrome" of blaming DAP for everything.
He said he expected more from his former ally.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dubai luxury hotels woo staycationers as tourists flee
Article:
DUBAI: Once reserved for wealthy travellers, Dubai's top-notch hotels have become almost exclusively reliant on residents, drawn in by dizzying staycation offers as war drives tourists away.
On the Palm, an artificial island that has become synonymous with Dubai opulence, five-star hotels are busy on weekends and holidays once more, despite having been deserted by tourists.
The clientele is driven by hotels offering residents-only deals that have become a lifeline for Dubai's luxury tourism.
"I had never been in a hotel on the Palm because the prices were crazy," said Fadi Iskandarani, a doctor in his sixties who just spent his first weekend at a luxury resort on the tree-shaped island.
The Lebanese national, who has lived in Dubai for five years, decided to opt for a staycation after he saw that a hotel on the Palm had slashed its rates by a factor of four.
The hotel was not packed, with some floors closed because there were not enough guests.
But the pool-side area was filled with people, he said, who came to the Palm to enjoy a slice of luxury that had long been unattainable.
"Luxury in Dubai has become affordable for residents, before it was just for the rich, very rich people," he said.
A lifeline
With 19.5 million yearly tourists, Dubai is among the region's top destinations and was long seen as a playground for the world's rich and famous.
Its 827 hotels - including 173 five-star establishments - boasted an average occupancy rate of more than 80 per cent.
But the war, which was triggered by US-Israeli strikes on Iran on Feb 28, shattered the Gulf's image as a haven of stability, with Tehran directing its fury at the oil-rich region.
The United Arab Emirates bore the brunt of Iran's missiles and drones, which hit hotels, including on the Palm, as well as its landmark Burj Al Arab resort.
Since a shaky ceasefire came into effect on Apr 8 some tourists have trickled in, but hotels are mostly relying on local guests, said Michael Robinson, the general manager of the Anantara The Palm Dubai Resort.
With its overwater villas, artificial lagoons and Thai-inspired decor, the luxury hotel is packed with Dubai residents, who get special discounts of up to 50 per cent.
On Fridays and Saturdays, hotel occupancy sits between 70 to 90 per cent, he said.
Sunday through to Thursday, it has an average occupancy of around 20 to 30 per cent.
This new clientele has offered hotels a lifeline, allowing Anantara The Palm to remain "cash positive" without resorting to layoffs.
'No return'
But staycations are not enough on the long term.
"Your staycation business is essentially one to two nights and that's it... Whereas previously, the international market, they might come for one week," Robinson said.
Should tourists stay away come July, when schools are closed and many families return home for the summer, "there won't be as many people wishing to do staycations", he said.
Some hotels, including Burj Al Arab, have temporarily closed for renovations as business slowed.
Others have cut staff or salaries, particularly hotels in downtown Dubai, which are dependent on business tourism.
An employee at one Dubai hotel, who spoke on condition of anonymity, said his salary had been cut by up to 40 per cent during and after the war, before it returned to normal in recent weeks.
Another employee at a hotel in neighbouring Abu Dhabi said he was put on unpaid leave for two months but that he is set to return to work soon with his salary restored.
Talks to end the war have dragged on for two months and sporadic strikes still punctuate life in the Gulf, straining tourism.
Yet Robinson remains hopeful.
"If we see some form of resolution in the next month or so... I think you'll see tourists come back faster than everyone anticipates," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Etihad Airways orders widebody planes, sees return to pre-war capacity in June
Article:
RIO DE JANEIRO: Etihad Airways is ordering more widebody planes as the Middle East carrier expects to be flying about eight per cent more than a year ago by June 15, chief executive officer (CEO) Antonoaldo Neves said in an interview.
He said on the sidelines of a global gathering of airline CEOs in Brazil on Saturday that the Abu Dhabi carrier is buying widebody planes in the double digits, declining to specify further.
Etihad is restoring flights after making cuts in March as the US-Israeli war on Iran turned regional, raising fuel prices, Neves said.
He said Etihad does not plan to trim costs by cutting flights at the moment.
"The biggest cost we have is an empty plane," he said. "So the way I cut cost is I don't have empty planes."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Body of elderly man found in drain, death ruled as drowning
Article:
KEPALA BATAS: The body of a 64-year-old man was found in a drain at Kampung Kolam, Jalan Merbau Kudung here yesterday, with an injury to his left ankle.
Seberang Perai Utara district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuar Abdul Rahman said police received a report on the discovery from a witness who was on his way to fish nearby.
"Investigations found that the victim was last seen by a witness about two days before the incident. Checks at the scene did not reveal any clues or exhibits that could assist the investigation," he said in a statement today.
Anuar said the post-mortem found the cause of death to be drowning, with no criminal elements detected.
The injury to the left ankle is believed to have been caused by a fall into the drain.
Those with information can contact investigating officer Sergeant Major Jefri Hussain at 04-5762222 extension 2275, or visit the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fire destroys 80pct of Yong Peng MCA office, disrupts Johor election preparations [WATCH]
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A fire that destroyed about 80 per cent of the MCA office in Yong Peng yesterday has disrupted the party's preparations for the upcoming Johor state election.
MCA Youth chief Ling Tian Soon said the party is considering relocating its operations to temporary premises once investigations by the Fire and Rescue Department are completed.
"This incident will affect MCA because the building has long served as the party's operations centre. We may need to find a temporary location to continue our preparations for the state election," he said.
Ling, who is also the Yong Peng assemblyman, said the blaze destroyed the building's public address system, newly installed air-conditioning units, furniture, and various documents and equipment used for community programmes.
He said the loss was deeply felt by the local Chinese community, many of whom regard the 70-year-old building as both a landmark and a centre for community activities.
"Everyone feels saddened by the loss of an iconic building in Yong Peng. I ask the public to remain patient," he said.
Ling said MCA would make plans moving forward to ensure it continues serving as a centre for community and family activities in the area.
According to Ling, the building has hosted a wide range of community activities, including meetings, dance classes, exercise sessions, lion dance training, and karaoke programmes.
"Although the fire mainly spread through the upper section of the building, the entire interior is no longer usable, and all activities have been temporarily suspended," he said.
The Fire and Rescue Department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 8.28pm yesterday, and firefighters brought the blaze under control and fully extinguished it by 9.56pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police record statements from Jaslinda's rescuers
Article:
TAPAH: Police have recorded statements from several Orang Asli villagers who rescued hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, after she went missing for 14 days in the Gunung Batu Putih area.
District police chief Superintendent Abdul Malik Hasim said those questioned included Nazri Bah Eng, 51, and two friends who found the victim in a forested area near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, yesterday.
"Police are also waiting for Jaslinda's condition to stabilise to enable us to record her statement and complete investigations into the case," he said when contacted today.
He said the statements were important to assist investigations and provide a complete picture of the victim's condition and location when she was found.
Malik said Jaslinda is still receiving treatment at Tapah Hospital after being brought down from the forest by rescuers yesterday evening.
"We were told that the victim has been confirmed to have sustained an injury to her left ankle," he said.
Jaslinda was reported missing while hiking in the Gunung Batu Putih area on May 24, prompting a large-scale search and rescue operation involving security agencies and volunteers.
She was found safe by a group of Orang Asli villagers at 5pm yesterday before being brought out for medical treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar heads to Japan for three-day visit, talks with Takaichi
Article:
TOKYO: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will undertake a three-day official visit to Japan, during which he is scheduled to hold bilateral talks with his Japanese counterpart Sanae Takaichi.
Japanese Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara was quoted by broadcaster NHK World as saying that the two leaders are expected to discuss a range of bilateral and international issues.
These include developments in the Middle East, as well as cooperation in security and economic matters.
Kihara said Japan and Malaysia had developed strong ties as comprehensive strategic partners that share common values and principles.
He added that relations with Malaysia were important to advancing Japan's vision of a Free and Open Indo-Pacific.
Anwar is also scheduled to deliver a keynote address at the 31st Nikkei International Conference on the Future of Asia on June 10.
He is expected to open the conference's main session at 9.40am local time (8.40am Malaysian time).
The conference, themed "Where Great Minds Shape Tomorrow", brings together political leaders, business executives and international experts to discuss Asia's economic outlook, geopolitical developments and future growth.
Among the regional leaders and dignitaries expected to attend are Timor-Leste President Jose Ramos-Horta, Laos Prime Minister Sonexay Siphandone, Thailand Deputy Prime Minister Sihasak Phuangketkeow and Vietnam Deputy Prime Minister Le Tien Chau.
Malaysian Ambassador to Japan Datuk Shahril Effendi Abd Ghany is scheduled to hold a press conference at the Malaysian Embassy in Tokyo tomorrow to provide further details on Anwar's visit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police tracking suspects in viral MRR2 cable theft case
Article:
AMPANG JAYA: Police are still tracking down a group of men believed to be involved in the cable theft along the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) that went viral.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said investigations are ongoing and efforts to identify and locate the suspects are being intensified.
"So far, no arrests have been made, and investigations are being carried out," he said in a statement today.
Yesterday, it was
reported
that police were tracking down four men seen stealing utility cables along the MRR2 in a video that has gone viral.
A witness saw the men loading cables into a grey car on Thursday and lodged a police report.
Checks revealed that the car's registration number belonged to another vehicle.
Investigations are being carried out under Section 431A of the Penal Code for mischief by damaging cables used for telegraphy and Section 235 of the Communications and Multimedia Act.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls crucial for state's future direction and people's well-being, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The upcoming Johor election is an important platform to determine the state's future direction and the people's well-being.
Pakatan Harapan (PH) communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said this message was conveyed during the launch of the coalition's election machinery for the state election in Batu Pahat last night, which was attended by more than 5,000 party workers and residents.
Fahmi said PH chairman and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed during the event that the election was not his wish, and the primary focus must remain on initiatives for the people.
In the same spirit, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke also emphasised that holding the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections simultaneously is important to ensure continuity and political stability in the country, he said in a Facebook post.
Fahmi, who is also the communications minister, said Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu reminded all parties of the importance of alignment between the state administration and the federal government.
"Johor has enormous potential. Planned development must genuinely benefit the majority of the people," Fahmi said.
The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 1 to pave the way for the state election.
The Election Commission will hold a special meeting on June 12 to discuss key dates for the polls, including the issuance of the election writ, nomination day, and polling day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aminolhuda apologises for bury BN remark, says history shows defeat possible
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Pakatan Harapan chairman Aminolhuda Hassan has apologised for his controversial remark that the coalition would bury Barisan Nasional for a second time in Johor, but maintained that history has shown BN can be defeated at the polls.
Aminolhuda said the comment was made out of shock after Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that BN would contest all 56 state assembly seats independently despite being partners in the federal government.
He apologised for the harsh tone but noted that BN was defeated during the 14th General Election in 2018, adding that a repeat remains possible.
Aminolhuda said PH is confident facing the upcoming Johor election due to its strongholds and loyal voters, while fence-sitters will judge the current political stability, rapid economic recovery, and infrastructure development.
Among the achievements highlighted were the government's efforts to manage living costs through initiatives such as the Rahmah Sales Programme, as well as maintaining one of the world's lowest RON95 petrol prices despite global energy market volatility.
He also credited the federal government for driving major development projects in Johor, including the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), the revival of Forest City, the Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, the Kuala Lumpur-Johor Bahru Electric Train Service (ETS) double-tracking project, and rail links to Pasir Gudang Port and Port of Tanjung Pelepas.
Addressing the potential split in Malay votes in multi-cornered fights, Aminolhuda said the core concerns for Malay voters are the cost of living, job opportunities, and security.
He said federal cash aid programmes such as Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR), Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara), and early schooling assistance have given Malay voters, who are the primary recipients, confidence in the government.
For young voters who make up 45 to 50 per cent of the electorate, he said the PH manifesto will focus on job opportunities, housing, cost of living, and education assistance.
He also questioned the need for an early state election when the Umno-BN state government already held a two-thirds majority.
He acknowledged that opponents would likely exploit the decision by PH and BN to contest separately in Johor, but insisted the partnership within the federal government remained intact.
"We remain committed to the Madani Government at the federal level and to maintaining political stability for the country's prosperity and security," he said.
Aminolhuda added that PH would conduct its campaign respectfully while focusing on presenting better policies and development plans for Johor, rather than engaging in personal attacks.
He also argued that Johoreans had shown their willingness to embrace political change before, citing BN's defeat in 2018 and PH's continued support in several parliamentary constituencies during the 2022 general election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Reject racism, religious bigotry, and corruption, Anwar tells youth
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI:  Malaysian youth must reject racism, religious bigotry, and corruption to lead the nation forward with unity, conviction, and clarity of purpose.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the country's future depended on a new generation of Malaysians who remained united, focused, and determined to build a stronger nation.
"This is a great country. And you, my children, can guarantee and ensure that this will be a greater nation if you remain united, focused, and with clarity of purpose.
"Enough of racism. Enough of religious bigotry. Enough of endemic corruption," he said.
Anwar said this when addressing students at AIMST University here, where he presented an annual government grant of RM25 million.
He said young Malaysians should be the force that changes the country's direction and ensures it never falters in its pursuit of progress and national unity.
He described Malaysia as a nation blessed by its diversity, with people of different races, religions, cultures and backgrounds living together in peace despite occasional tensions and controversies.
"There may be noise here and there, but the country remains peaceful. That is our strength," he added.
Anwar, however, stressed that preserving harmony in a diverse society required maturity and wisdom.
"I am not saying it is easy, but what we need is maturity and wisdom," he stressed.
Anwar also announced an additional RM1 million allocation to assist students from poor families at AIMST University, on top of existing federal government support.
He said the Madani government was committed to ensuring that underprivileged students were not denied access to higher education because of financial constraints.
He also highlighted a recent decision by the government to provide RM100 to every secondary school and university student for educational materials and textbooks.
While acknowledging that the assistance might not be sufficient to meet all needs, he described it as a meaningful start to encourage students to continue pursuing their studies.
Anwar said he was encouraged by the optimism and aspirations shown by students, whom he described as the country's greatest hope.
He urged them to make full use of educational opportunities and embrace the values of unity, integrity and perseverance.
"You should determine the future of this nation. We must lead with conviction and with tenacity to make sure that we never falter," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Don't be lulled into complacency despite strong economic performance, Anwar warns
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Malaysians should not be lulled into complacency despite strong economic indicators, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
He said education and integrity remained critical challenges facing the nation.
He said the national economy remained resilient despite global geopolitical turmoil, including conflicts in the Middle East and disruptions affecting international trade routes.
He noted that the ringgit was among the strongest-performing currencies in Asia, while the country's stock market and economic growth continued to hold firm amid global uncertainties.
Anwar said Malaysia's unemployment rate was among the lowest in its history, while foreign direct investment and domestic investment had reached record highs.
"Of course, we must be grateful, but we should not be lulled into complacency. We must see the challenges ahead," he said in his speech after presenting the annual government grant to AIMST University here today.
He also announced an additional RM1 million allocation to assist students from poor families, on top of existing federal government support.
Anwar identified education as one of the country's biggest priorities, stressing the need to ensure Malaysia continued to excel in developing future generations.
Anwar said this was reflected in the government's budget priorities, with education receiving the largest allocation under his administration.
He said Malaysia was unusual in placing education above all other sectors, as many countries allocated the largest share of public spending to defence.
"Since I became Finance Minister, education has consistently received the highest allocation, followed by healthcare," he added.
Anwar also reaffirmed the government's commitment to ensuring equal access to quality education for all Malaysians, regardless of race, social background or geographical location, stressing that the ultimate measure of success for any educational institution is not its infrastructure or funding, but the impact it has on students and their prospects.
Addressing the broader national development agenda, he stressed that the principle of justice and equal opportunity remained central to the Madani framework.
He noted that political discourse in Malaysia often focused on race, social groups and wealth distribution, while overlooking the importance of providing every child — whether from rural villages, estates, urban fringes or remote areas — with a fair chance to succeed through education.
He described this as the "democratisation of access to quality education."
Anwar emphasised that quality education should transcend racial and family backgrounds, describing it as a key investment in the nation's future.
Reflecting on his personal connection to education, Anwar said he often felt sentimental when visiting universities and engaging with students and academics.
Although he currently serves as prime minister, he remarked that he sometimes wonders whether he should have remained in academia instead of entering politics.
Nevertheless, he said his role in government allowed him to help shape policies that secure a better future for the nation's youth.
"Justice must remain the fundamental principle guiding Malaysia's development, echoing themes he recently raised at the Bumiputera Economic Congress," he said.
Anwar acknowledged that all governments, including his own administration, had weaknesses and were open to criticism.
However, he stressed that one principle must never be compromised.
"When you become leaders, do not use power to rob the wealth of the nation and its people," he said.
Anwar said abuse of power and corruption would ultimately undermine the country's future, regardless of how strong its economy became.
Quoting Mahatma Gandhi, he said the world had enough resources to meet everyone's needs but not enough to satisfy human greed.
He urged the younger generation to uphold integrity and responsible leadership to safeguard Malaysia's long-term prosperity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Johor, Negri Sembilan politics must focus on justice, not racial divides
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Political contests in Johor and Negri Sembilan should centre on justice and equal opportunities, not racial rhetoric or divisive politics, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
Both states recently
dissolved
their respective state assemblies, paving the way for
state elections
.
The prime minister said political debates often became consumed by questions of race, status and wealth, while neglecting the future of ordinary Malaysians and their children.
"Public discourse should not be reduced to contests over who could stoke racial sentiments, exploit majority-minority narratives or spread hatred.
"Instead, the focus should be on justice, equality and humanity," he said in his speech after presenting annual government grant to AIMST University here today.
He also announced an additional RM1 million allocation to assist students from poor families, on top of existing federal government support.
Anwar said the fundamental challenge for the country was ensuring equitable access to quality education for all, regardless of background, ethnicity, or where they lived.
"It is not about race and it is not about your family background. It is about our future as a nation and the future of your families," he said.
Anwar said true justice was not merely about providing equal treatment, but ensuring fair opportunities for those from disadvantaged backgrounds.
Drawing on the ideas of Harvard political philosopher Michael Joseph Sandel, he said justice must be rooted in fairness.
"You cannot compare the son of a multimillionaire who has access to everything with the son of a poor estate worker or farmer and then say the outcome defines justice," he said.
Anwar also cited Indian statesman Dr B.R. Ambedkar as an example of how determination and access to education could transform lives.
"Despite coming from one of the most marginalised communities in India, Ambedkar rose to study at leading universities and later chaired the committee that drafted India's Constitution," he said.
Anwar said Ambedkar's life demonstrated the importance of social democracy and equal opportunities, particularly for the poor and disadvantaged.
He also referenced French political thinker Alexis de Tocqueville, saying democracy could not flourish through hatred, prejudice or ignorance.
"To strengthen freedom and democracy, we must elevate humanity and human dignity," he said.
In this respect, Anwar urged students to remain proud of their roots and appreciate the sacrifices made by parents from humble backgrounds, including estate workers, farmers and others who struggled to provide opportunities for their children.
He said many ordinary Malaysians possessed values of compassion and humanity that could serve as lessons even for political leaders and intellectuals.
He also encouraged students to read the works of thinkers such as Ambedkar, Sandel and Tocqueville to better understand the principles of justice, democracy and humanity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PN acknowledges Johor MB's strong image but eyes gains in next state polls
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has acknowledged that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi enjoys a strong public image and is widely seen as an effective administrator, but believes the coalition can increase its seat count in the next state election.
State PN chairman Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal said while Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is seen as active and performing well, a leader's individual popularity does not reflect the overall performance of the state government.
Sahruddin said Bersatu will contest under the PN ticket alongside Pas, Gerakan, the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP), and Ikatan Prihatin Rakyat (IPR).
He dismissed rumours of tension between Bersatu and Pas in the state, describing their relationship as strong and focused on election machinery preparations.
On seat negotiations, Sahruddin said allocations will be decided by consensus based on the respective strengths of each component party in the constituencies to maximise their chances of winning.
He said the coalition will address key public grievances during the campaign, including the cost of living, utility tariffs, taxes, and other bread-and-butter issues requiring state intervention.
Sahruddin also noted that the state administration relies too heavily on a single figure, whereas voters prefer a strong leadership team with a clear succession plan.
Commenting on Barisan Nasional (BN) deciding to contest solo despite its cooperation with Pakatan Harapan (PH) at the federal level, Sahruddin said voters are mature enough to judge the inconsistency between federal and state alignments.
He said PN will remain focused on offering better policies, alternative leadership, and concrete solutions for the people of Johor rather than worrying about its opponents.
In the 15th Johor election in 2022, PN won three out of the 56 state assembly seats, with Bersatu taking two and Pas winning one.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Google paying rival Musk US$920mil a month for data centre space
Article:
SAN FRANCISCO: Elon Musk's space company SpaceX will collect US$920 million a month from Google in the coming years for renting its computer power to its giant tech competitor, reported German news agency dpa.
Google needs the computer capacity for its artificial intelligence (AI) services. The agreement runs from October this year until the end of June 2029.
In a similar deal, rival AI company Anthropic is also buying computing power from SpaceX for US$1.25 billion a month under a multi-year agreement.
Musk folded his own AI company xAI into SpaceX and built several data centres for the firm. After the arrangement with Anthropic announced a few weeks ago, he said one of the data centres would be sufficient for SpaceX's AI activities.
The AI chatbot Grok is being developed at xAI and now SpaceX. Grok is used less than OpenAI's ChatGPT, Anthropic's Claude and Google's rival software Gemini.
By renting urgently needed computing power to Anthropic and Google, Musk is also helping the companies in their competition with rival OpenAI. Musk, a former co-founder of Open AI, has been feuding with the firm for years.
AI infrastructure is expensive. Google alone has indicated capital spending of up to US$190 billion for this year, which is mainly intended for data centres.
SpaceX plans to go public next week and is seeking a total valuation of US$1.7 trillion as well as record revenues of US$75 billion.
SpaceX's pure business figures stand in stark contrast to the targeted stock market valuation, with investors expected to pay more for the hope of future successes.
Last year, the company posted losses of around US$4.94 billion on revenue of US$18.67 billion. The rental of the data centres alone is set to improve these figures significantly.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perikatan on the brink? PAS to decide fate of Bersatu alliance in Monday meeting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The future of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition hangs in the balance as PAS will convene a high-stakes central working committee (CWC) meeting tomorrow night to determine its ongoing relationship with Bersatu.
PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed the meeting today, when speaking to reporters following the launch of a book on the Iran-Israel war at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair,
Free Malaysia Today
reported.
“It will be tomorrow, God willing, Monday. We will have a meeting at night,” Hadi said, addressing mounting speculation over when the party leadership would formally decide the trajectory of the PAS-Bersatu partnership.
The alliance between the two PN pillars has been tenuous since Tan Sri Muhyddin Yassin was forced to resign as the chairman of the coalition last year.
The rift widened after Hadi publicly signalled that PAS would reassess its cooperation with Bersatu, delivering a pointed warning that the party’s “patience has its limits.”
At the core of the dispute are several high-friction issues that have eroded trust between the two parties.
Chief among these is Bersatu’s move to unseat the Perlis menteri besar, as well as allegations of Bersatu interfering in state-level appointments in the PAS strongholds of Kedah and Kelantan.
Adding to the tension is a strategic disagreement over the coalition's growth; Hadi alleged that Bersatu has consistently opposed the admission of new Malay-Muslim parties into the PN fold whereas the Islamist party has continued its outreach towards such groups.

Title: Govt approves RM267m to keep Bomba on 100pc standby ‘at all times’ for emergencies
Article:
TELUK INTAN, June 7 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) has allocated substantial funds to ensure the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) remains fully prepared for emergencies at all times.
Minister Nga Kor Ming said the ministry has approved RM267 million this year to upgrade equipment for search and rescue missions.
“Under the 12th Malaysia Plan, we also approved RM1.3 billion for the department to acquire critical assets, because we never know when a disaster will strike.
“For that reason, the Fire and Rescue Department has been ordered to remain on 100 per cent standby at all times,” he told reporters today during a briefing on the Sungai Perak Phase 1 Integrated River Basin Development Project and other related works in the Hilir Perak district.
He was responding to a question about strengthening JBPM’s search and rescue operations, especially following the case of climber Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who was found safe near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu in Tapah yesterday.
Nga said the ministry will provide written details on the estimated costs of Jaslinda’s search and rescue mission.
“What matters most is that she was found. That’s our priority. Of course, every mission has weaknesses. We will find the causes and fix them,” he said when asked about communication issues, which was one of the challenges in the search.
Jaslinda was part of a climbing expedition with 13 others and two Forestry Mountain Guides on the Trans Spencer Chapman trail.
The climb began at 2am on May 23, starting from Pos Gedung through Gunung Bah Gading, Gunung Batu Putih, to Kuala Woh.
She and another climber, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, reportedly stopped due to health issues.
However, the supply chain executive is believed to have continued towards the peak and was reported missing after she was last sighted on May 24. — Bernama

Title: Upko’s application to join Sabah ruling coalition set for decision on June 18
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, June 7 — Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) is expected to decide on the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation's (Upko) application to join the coalition as a component party on June 18.
GRS secretary-general Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said he is currently conducting engagement sessions with Upko leaders to gather the necessary information before preparing a paper for consideration at the coalition's Supreme Council meeting on that date.
“The GRS meeting is scheduled to take place on June 18 and an official decision is expected to be made then,” he told reporters after officiating the Ihsan Food Bank (i-FB) Initiative programme here today.
Armizan, who is also Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister, described Upko's application as a positive development towards uniting Sabah-based parties under a common political platform.
He said a key condition for Upko's admission is its commitment to GRS’ four core principles: Sabah First, Sabah Forward, Sabah Prosper and Sabah United.
On May 29, Upko formally submitted its application to GRS chairman Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, who is also Sabah Chief Minister, through its president, Datuk Ewon Benedick.
GRS currently comprises five component parties: Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah, Parti Bersatu Sabah, Liberal Democratic Party, Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah and Parti Cinta Sabah. — Bernama

Title: The highway crawl begins: Slow afternoon traffic as holidaymakers return to the Klang Valley
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — Traffic flow on several major highways leading to Kuala Lumpur was reported to be slow as of 2pm today as motorists began returning home on the final day of the mid-year school holidays and following recent double public holidays.
A spokesman for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said the increase in traffic volume was expected as road users made their journeys back to the capital after spending the holiday period in their hometowns and vacation destinations.
Despite the higher number of vehicles on the road, he said the overall traffic situation remained under control, with no reports of extraordinary congestion or major incidents.
On the North-South Expressway (PLUS) E1 southbound, traffic was reported to be moving slowly from Seberang Jaya to Prai, as well as from Bandar Cassia to Jawi.
For the PLUS E2 route, traffic flow was also slow from Senawang to Port Dickson, and after the Sungai Besi Toll Plaza heading towards the city centre, he said when contacted by Bernama.
Meanwhile, on the Kuala Lumpur–Karak Expressway (KLK) heading towards Kuala Lumpur, slow-moving traffic was detected from Gambang to Sri Jaya and along the stretch between Lentang and Bukit Tinggi.
As a proactive measure to ease congestion and improve traffic flow, highway authorities have activated Smart Lanes, which utilise emergency lanes at several strategic locations.
Regarding traffic flow out of the capital towards the northern and southern regions, it was reported to be smooth with no significant disruptions.
Motorists are advised to remain patient, drive cautiously and comply with traffic instructions issued by the authorities throughout their journey. — Bernama

Title: Outgoing J-Kom chief Ismail Yusop thanks staff, says new responsibility awaits
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — Department of Community Communications (J-Kom) Director-General Datuk Ismail Yusop has announced the end of his service after more than a year leading the department, saying he will soon take on a new assignment.
In a Facebook post, Ismail, affectionately known as “Tok Abah”, said every journey in life has its moments of meeting and parting, and that every ending opens the door to a new beginning.
“Today is my last day serving as the Director-General of J-Kom. Insya-Allah, I will carry out a new responsibility at a new place for religion, race and country with the same spirit and determination,” he said.
Ismail, a former deputy director (training) at the National Civics Bureau (AKM), was appointed to the post on Jan 15 last year to fill the vacancy left by Dr Mohd Khairuddin Othman, whose service was terminated in September 2023.
Mohd Khairuddin, also Selangor PKR secretary, was appointed J-Kom Director-General on March 1, 2023, replacing Prof Datuk Dr Mohammad Agus Yusof.
Ismail said leading J-Kom had been a meaningful experience and expressed gratitude for the achievements recorded since January 2025 until now.
“If there is anything that can be considered an achievement, it is not the result of an individual effort, but rather the spirit of teamwork, loyalty and commitment of all J-Kom staff,” he said.
He also extended his appreciation to all department staff, strategic partners, community leaders and all parties who had placed their trust, support and cooperation in him throughout his tenure.
“I also seek forgiveness for any shortcomings during my service and pray for the best for everyone. Thank you for all the memories, experiences and friendships along this journey,” he added.
J-Kom plays a role in ensuring government policies are effectively communicated at community level, while its functions have been refined to strengthen inter-ethnic relations through its programmes and activities. — Bernama

Title: Anwar greenlights RM25m annual grant for AIMST, plus RM1m top-up for needy students
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI, June 7 — The federal government has approved an additional RM1 million allocation for AIMST University to ensure students from underprivileged families continue to receive education assistance.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the additional allocation would supplement the annual grant given to the private university.
“The Madani Government will ensure that the poorest students at this university continue to receive guaranteed assistance. Therefore, I am adding RM1 million specifically for that purpose.
“Last week, we announced an allocation of RM100 for every secondary school and university student to purchase books,” he said when speaking at the annual government grant presentation ceremony for AIMST University in Bedong, near here, today.
Also present were Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir and MIC president Tan Sri Dr SA Vigneswaran, who is also AIMST University chancellor and chairman.At the ceremony, AIMST University received an annual grant amounting to RM25 million.
Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said that despite various global challenges, the country's economic growth remains resilient and strong.
He said the national unemployment rate is currently at its lowest level, while investment performance has recorded an all-time high in the country's history.
“We are certainly grateful, but as I always say, 'shouldn't be lulled into complacency'. See the challenges. One of the major challenges, of course, is education; how to ensure that we continue to excel,” he said.
The Prime Minister said education is an important instrument in uplifting the dignity of the people and building a better future.
Therefore, he said the government places education as a key component that receives the highest allocation in the Budget every year. — Bernama

Title: RM205m lifeline for MSMEs as Putrajaya unveils new business support initiatives
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 7 — The Ministry of Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development (Kuskop), through SME Corp Malaysia, has launched an industry and business support programme (Sinar) to assist micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs).
Its minister, Steven Sim Chee Keong, said the Sinar programme offers financial assistance totalling RM205 million through four new initiatives for MSMEs nationwide to help expand their businesses.
“In the face of global economic uncertainty, the government cannot allow MSMEs to confront these challenges alone. Through Sinar, we aim to ensure that entrepreneurs have access to the financing and support they need to survive, grow, and create employment opportunities,” he added.
He was speaking to reporters at the closing ceremony of the Empowering Malaysian Businesses Carnival at the Angsana Johor Bahru Mall today. Also present were Deputy Minister of Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development, Datuk Mohamad Alamin, and Kuskop Secretary-General, Datuk Seri Khairul Dzaimee Daud.
Elaborating further, he said Sinar is a new Kuskop programme implemented through SME Corp Malaysia, encompassing five new initiatives involving financial assistance.
According to him, three financing programmes are being offered with funding of up to RM5 million at profit or interest rates ranging from 3 per cent to 5 per cent per annum through collaborations with Malaysian Industrial Development Finance Bhd (MIDF), microLEAP PLT, and Funding Societies.
“In addition to financing, the Inclusive Business Scaling Programme is a capacity-building initiative that provides grants of up to 90 per cent for SMEs. This is aimed at integrating inclusive communities into their supply chains and helping micro-enterprises develop relevant skills to transform their businesses to a higher level,” he said.
To facilitate access to these initiatives, he added that MSMEs applying for financing only need to obtain MSME Status, an official certification confirming that a business meets the MSME definition.
A 50 per cent discount on MSME Status applications is being offered from this month until Aug 31. — Bernama

Title: ‘UPSI Berduka’: Book, fund and new crisis management guide follow Gerik bus crash that killed 15 varsity students
Article:
PASIR PUTEH, June 7 — Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) has published a book titled
UPSI Berduka
, documenting the tragic road accident involving its students in Gerik, Perak, as a reference and guide for future crisis management and emergency response efforts.
UPSI vice-chancellor Prof Datuk Dr Md Amin Md Taff said the publication, which took nearly 10 months to complete, details the chronology of the tragedy, the seven phases of crisis management undertaken by the university in collaboration with various agencies, and personal accounts from those directly affected by the incident.
He said the book serves as a comprehensive record of the experiences of victims’ families, survivors, volunteers, rescue personnel, frontline workers and other parties involved throughout the management of the tragedy.
“This book does not merely recount the experiences of one group, but brings together the voices of the many individuals involved from the first day of the tragedy until the completion of the various processes concerning the victims and their families.
“We hope it will serve as a useful reference and guideline should a similar incident occur in the future, although we sincerely hope such a tragedy never happens again,” he told reporters after the “UPSI Tautan Kasih dan Ukhwah” programme with families affected by the Gerik tragedy in Tok Bali today.
Md Amin said the book is expected to be officially launched by Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir at the UPSI campus in the near future.
Meanwhile, he revealed that the “Tabung
UPSI Berduka
” fund, established in the aftermath of the tragedy, had successfully raised about RM2 million in contributions from various parties since the incident occurred.
The funds have been distributed in stages to all 42 affected victims and their next of kin, including injured students and the families of those who lost their lives.
